title: Settle the d = 7 slice of the forbidden out-degree orientation conjecture
generalization_signal: The pass isolates a reusable odd-degree template: remove a perfect matching, orient the even core Eulerian and strong, then orient the matching quotient strongly. This proves a strong {3,4}-orientation for every 7-regular graph and gives the full conjecture on bipartite 7-regular graphs, but the nonbipartite center-hitting repair step is still missing.
strongest_honest_claim: The bipartite 7-regular slice is correct but already covered by prior art; the claimed arbitrary-graph theorem for forbidden sets disjoint from {3,4} is not proved because the quotient bridge step fails.
candidate_theorem_slice: Known slice only: every bipartite 7-regular graph admits an F-avoiding orientation for every forbidden set F of size at most 3. No new frontier slice was verified in this artifact.
